summary: Iteration 4 backend verified end-to-end. 86/86 pytest tests passing (65 regression from iter 3 + 21 new iter-4 tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter4.py). All requested endpoints validated: GET /api/stats/monthly (shape + period.start=1st-of-month + period.end=today + IDR total + tx_count + po_count; accessible to admin/finance/store; 401 unauth); GET /api/deliveries/autocomplete (returns {destinations,drivers}, no empty strings, reflects newly-created delivery, finance can call, 401 unauth); POST /api/sales-orders/import/xlsx (admin+staff 200 with inserted count; duplicate so_no counted in skipped_duplicates on re-upload; finance 403; store 403); POST /api/deliveries with DeliveryItem.so_no stored and retrievable via GET list; SO access — store role GET /api/sales-orders 200 read-only, POST/PUT/DELETE all 403; POST /api/transactions/parse-po via Gemini 3 Flash (real PNG with PO text via Pillow → 200 with vendor_name/po_no/po_date/items/currency/exchange_rate fields; unauth 401; .txt 400 with 'Format tidak didukung'; empty file 400 'File kosong').
critical_code_review_comments: ai.py: /transactions/parse-po correctly validates auth, file size (10MB), empty file, and format before invoking LLM — good defense-in-depth. PDF path uses PyMuPDF (fitz) with dpi=200 which is a reasonable OCR resolution.
ai.py: temp file always cleaned in finally block — no leaks.
ai.py: _extract_json is tolerant of code-fenced Gemini output — pragmatic.
ai.py: currency defaults to IDR if invalid, exchange_rate forced to 1.0 for IDR — matches _compute_idr semantics in transactions.py. Consistent.
orders.py /sales-orders/import/xlsx: async find_one per row inside the loop is O(N) roundtrips; fine for small imports but could be batched via one $in query for very large uploads. Not blocking.
orders.py /deliveries/autocomplete: uses distinct() which is unindexed on 'destination'/'driver_name' — fine at current scale; add index later if collections grow.
transactions.py /stats/monthly: uses {$ifNull:['$total_price_idr','$total_price']} — correct fallback for legacy rows without IDR normalization.
models.py: DeliveryItem.so_no has default '' (not None) — matches how it's rendered on frontend list view. Good.
routers/ai.py imports emergentintegrations lazily inside the endpoint — avoids startup crash if lib missing. Good.

Potential issue (non-blocking): /transactions/parse-po does not restrict to write roles (staff/admin) — currently any authenticated user (incl. store/finance) can call and spend LLM quota. Consider switching from get_current_user to require_write if you want to lock this down.
test_credentials: susanto/admin123, erwin/erwin123, staff01/staff123, store01/store123, finance01/finance123 — all working
seed_data_creation: None. Test data created inline with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up in each test. TestZCleanup in backend_test.py sweeps any leftover TEST_ transactions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-4 tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_iter4.py — separate file from iter-3 backend_test.py. Real PNG for parse-po is generated at runtime via Pillow (DejaVuSans font on Debian). If EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is unset, test_valid_png_returns_fields will pytest.skip. Multipart uploads MUST NOT set Content-Type on the session (requests will set correct boundary) — the fixture in test_iter4.py omits it; do the same when adding more upload tests. /api/transactions/parse-po currently uses get_current_user (not require_write) so any authenticated user can call it.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. During test authoring, an initial mismatch (session Content-Type=application/json overriding multipart boundary → 422 'Field required') was fixed by not setting Content-Type on the login session.
